content:
# Numbers Chapter 35 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 193–194 | **Source Image:** `NUM_035_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_035_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0141–pa_0142

---

**1.** And YHWH spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan [at] Jericho, saying,

**2.** "Command the sons of Israel that they give to the Levites from the inheritance of their possession cities to dwell in; and pastureland for the cities all around them you shall give to the Levites.

**3.** And the cities shall be for them to dwell in, and their pasturelands shall be for their livestock and for their goods and for all their living creatures.

**4.** And the pasturelands of the cities which you shall give to the Levites [shall extend] from the wall of the city and outward one thousand cubits all around.

**5.** And you shall measure from outside the city on the eastern side two thousand cubits, and on the southern side two thousand cubits, and on the western side two thousand cubits, and on the northern side two thousand cubits, with the city in the middle. This shall be to them the pasturelands of the cities.

**6.** And the cities which you shall give to the Levites [shall be] the six cities of refuge which you shall give for the manslayer to flee there, and besides them you shall give forty-two cities.

**7.** All the cities which you shall give to the Levites [shall be] forty-eight cities — them and their pasturelands.

**8.** And the cities which you shall give from the possession of the sons of Israel: from the many you shall make many, and from the few you shall make few; each according to his inheritance which he inherits shall give from his cities to the Levites."

**9.** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**10.** "Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: When you cross the Jordan into the land of Canaan,

**11.** then you shall select for yourselves cities — cities of refuge they shall be for you — and the manslayer who strikes a person unintentionally shall flee there.

**12.** And the cities shall be for you a refuge from the avenger, and the manslayer shall not die until he stands before the congregation for judgment.

**13.** And the cities which you shall give, six cities of refuge they shall be for you.

**14.** Three of the cities you shall give from across the Jordan, and three of the cities you shall give in the land of Canaan; cities of refuge they shall be.

**15.** For the sons of Israel and for the sojourner and for the resident among them, these six cities shall be for a refuge, for anyone striking a person unintentionally to flee there.

**16.** And if with an instrument of iron he struck him and he died, he [is] a murderer; the murderer shall surely be put to death.

**17.** And if with a stone [in the] hand by which one could die he struck him and he died, he [is] a murderer; the murderer shall surely be put to death.

**18.** Or [if] with a wooden instrument [in the] hand by which one could die he struck him and he died, he [is] a murderer; the murderer shall surely be put to death.

**19.** The avenger of blood — he himself shall put to death the murderer; when he encounters him, he shall put him to death.

**20.** And if in hatred he pushed him, or threw at him [something] with intent, and he died,

**21.** or in enmity he struck him with his hand and he died, the one who struck [him] shall surely be put to death — he [is] a murderer; the avenger of blood shall put to death the murderer when he encounters him.

**22.** But if suddenly, without enmity, he pushed him, or threw upon him any instrument without intent,

**23.** or [with] any stone by which one could die, without seeing [him], and he caused [it] to fall upon him and he died, and he [was] not his enemy and [was] not seeking his harm,

**24.** then the congregation shall judge between the one who struck and the avenger of blood according to these judgments.

**25.** And the congregation shall deliver the manslayer from the hand of the avenger of blood, and the congregation shall return him to his city of refuge where he fled, and he shall dwell in it until the death of the high priest who was anointed with the holy oil.

**26.** But if the manslayer shall indeed go out beyond the border of his city of refuge where he flees,

**27.** and the avenger of blood finds him outside the border of his city of refuge, and the avenger of blood kills the manslayer — there is no blood-guilt upon him.

**28.** For in his city of refuge he shall dwell until the death of the high priest, and after the death of the high priest the manslayer shall return to the land of his possession.

**29.** And these things shall be for you as a statute of judgment throughout your generations in all your dwelling places.

**30.** Anyone who strikes a person — by [the] mouth of witnesses shall the murderer be put to death, but one witness shall not testify against a person to die.

**31.** And you shall not take a ransom for the life of a murderer who [is] guilty of death, for he shall surely be put to death.

**32.** And you shall not take a ransom for him to flee to his city of refuge, to return to dwell in the land before the death of the priest.

**33.** And you shall not pollute the land in which you [are], for the blood — it pollutes the land, and for the land no atonement can be made for the blood which is shed in it except by the blood of the one who shed it.

**34.** And you shall not defile the land in which you [are] dwelling, in the midst of which I [am] dwelling, for I, YHWH, [am] dwelling in the midst of the sons of Israel."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Verse 2 — "pastureland" (מִגְרָשׁ, *migrash*):** From the root גרש ("to drive out, expel"), the noun denotes open land surrounding a city used for driving livestock. Rendered consistently as "pastureland" rather than "suburbs" (KJV) or "open land" (NRSV). The LXX (Vaticanus) renders προάστεια ("outskirts, suburbs").

2. **Verses 4–5 — the measurement discrepancy:** Verse 4 prescribes one thousand cubits outward from the city wall; verse 5 prescribes two thousand cubits measured from outside the city on each side. The rabbinical resolution (b. Sotah 27b; Rashi) is that the one thousand cubits is open pastureland and the two thousand cubits includes an outer zone for fields and vineyards. The literal translation preserves both measurements without harmonization, as does the Leningrad Codex text. The Vaticanus LXX renders both figures faithfully (χιλίους and δισχιλίους).

3. **Verse 5 — "with the city in the middle" (וְהָעִיר בַּתָּוֶךְ):** This phrase confirms the city stands at the center of the measured area. The Hebrew בַּתָּוֶךְ ("in the midst") is spatial. The LXX reads καὶ ἡ πόλις μέσον τούτου.

4. **Verse 11 — "select for yourselves" (וְהִקְרִיתֶם, *vehiqritem*):** The Hiphil of קרה, literally "cause to meet/encounter" — hence "designate, select." This unusual verbal form for the appointment of refuge cities emphasizes divine providence in the arrangement. The Vaticanus reads καὶ διαστελεῖτε ὑμῖν αὐτοῖς πόλεις ("and you shall set apart for yourselves cities").

5. **Verse 12 — "the avenger" (הַגֹּאֵל, *hago'el*):** The Hebrew גֹּאֵל ("redeemer, kinsman-redeemer") is used here in its role as blood-avenger — the nearest male relative obligated to pursue the killer. Rendered "avenger" in context. The full phrase גֹּאֵל הַדָּם ("avenger of blood") appears in verses 19, 21, 24, 25, 27.

6. **Verse 16 — "shall surely be put to death" (מוֹת יוּמַת, *mot yumat*):** Infinitive absolute (מוֹת) + finite verb (יוּמַת), the emphatic legal formula of certain death. This construction appears in verses 16, 17, 18, 21, and 31. Preserved with the doubling "shall surely be put to death."

7. **Verse 19 — "when he encounters him" (בִּפְגְעוֹ־בוֹ, *bifge'o-vo*):** Literally "in his encountering him." The preposition בְּ with the infinitive construct of פגע conveys immediacy — the avenger acts upon meeting the murderer. The Vaticanus reads ὅταν συναντήσῃ αὐτῷ ("whenever he meets him").

8. **Verse 20 — "with intent" (בִּצְדִיָּה, *bitsediyyah*):** Literally "in lying-in-wait," from the root צדה. This denotes premeditation — intentional ambush. Rendered "with intent" to preserve the legal precision. The LXX renders ἐξ ἐνέδρου ("from ambush").

9. **Verse 25 — "the high priest who was anointed with the holy oil" (הַכֹּהֵן הַגָּדוֹל אֲשֶׁר־מָשַׁח אֹתוֹ בְּשֶׁמֶן הַקֹּדֶשׁ):** The full designation ties the refuge system to the priestly office. The death of the high priest functions as a kind of atonement or epoch-marker releasing the manslayer. The Vaticanus reads τοῦ ἱερέως τοῦ μεγάλου ὃν ἔχρισεν αὐτὸν τῷ ἐλαίῳ τῷ ἁγίῳ.

10. **Verse 26 — "shall indeed go out" (יָצֹא יֵצֵא, *yatso' yetse'*):** Another infinitive absolute construction — "going out, he goes out" — emphasizing the deliberate nature of the departure from the city of refuge. Rendered "shall indeed go out."

11. **Verse 27 — "there is no blood-guilt upon him" (אֵין לוֹ דָּם, *'ein lo dam*):** Literally "there is not to him blood." The terse legal formula declares the avenger free of guilt for killing the manslayer found outside refuge. The Vaticanus reads οὐκ ἔνοχός ἐστιν ("he is not liable").

12. **Verse 30 — "by the mouth of witnesses" (לְפִי עֵדִים, *lefi 'edim*):** Literally "according to the mouth of witnesses." Hebrew פִּי ("mouth") is used idiomatically for testimony. The requirement of multiple witnesses (plural עֵדִים) establishes a minimum of two, confirmed by Deuteronomy 17:6; 19:15. The Vaticanus reads ἐπὶ μαρτύρων ("upon witnesses").

13. **Verse 31 — "ransom" (כֹּפֶר, *kofer*):** From the root כפר ("to cover, atone"), כֹּפֶר is a payment that covers or substitutes for a life. The law forbids accepting any monetary substitute for a convicted murderer's life. This prohibition is unique in the ancient Near East, where other legal codes permitted pecuniary substitution for homicide.

14. **Verse 33 — "pollute" (חנף, *chanaf*) vs. verse 34 "defile" (טמא, *tame'*):** Two distinct Hebrew roots are used. חנף denotes moral pollution or profanation of the land through bloodshed. טמא denotes ritual impurity or defilement. The translation preserves this distinction: "pollute" for חנף and "defile" for טמא. The Vaticanus uses φονοκτονέω ("commit murder") for the first and βδελύσσω ("make abominable") for a related concept, though the Greek rendering is less precise than the Hebrew distinction.

15. **Verse 34 — "I, YHWH, [am] dwelling" (אֲנִי יהוה שֹׁכֵן):** The theological climax of the chapter: YHWH's own dwelling among the sons of Israel is the ultimate reason the land must not be defiled by unavenged blood. The participial form שֹׁכֵן ("dwelling") describes YHWH's ongoing, continuous presence. The Vaticanus reads ὅτι ἐγὼ κύριος κατασκηνῶ ("for I, Lord, am encamping/dwelling").

16. **Vaticanus comparison — general:** The LXX text in Vaticanus for Numbers 35 follows the Hebrew closely throughout. The legal terminology is rendered with standard Greek equivalents. No major pluses or minuses are observed relative to the MT. Minor differences include the LXX's occasional use of clarifying phrases (e.g., adding "these" or "to you" for explicitness) and the expected variation in transliteration of technical terms. The order and content of all thirty-four verses correspond between MT and LXX.
